landing.aspirePromise: Aspire is a developer-first, always-free, open-source platform. Join a thriving community and help shape the future of modern development.

01.heading: Agent-ready
01.text: Aspire is an agent-ready, code-first tool for composing, debugging, and deploying distributed applications.

02.heading: Developer-first
02.text: Aspire is a developer-first, open-source platform for building, running, and deploying distributed apps.

03.heading: Whole stack
03.text: Aspire is a code-first way to define services, databases, containers, and dependencies together in one place.

04.heading: One command
04.text: Aspire is a local-first orchestration toolchain that runs your full distributed app with a single command.

05.heading: Polyglot
05.text: Aspire is a polyglot platform for orchestrating apps built with C#, JavaScript, Python, Java, Go, and more.

06.heading: Smart defaults
06.text: Aspire is an opinionated app model that handles connection strings, ports, dependencies, and startup order.

07.heading: OpenTelemetry
07.text: Aspire is observability-first, with built-in logs, metrics, traces, and health checks through OpenTelemetry.

08.heading: Portable architecture
08.text: Aspire is one architecture definition that works locally, in CI/CD, and across Kubernetes and cloud hosts.

09.heading: Extensible ecosystem
09.text: Aspire is extensible by design, with integrations for databases, caches, cloud services, and developer tools.

10.heading: AppHost control plane
10.text: Aspire is a unifying control plane where the AppHost models distributed systems as code, not YAML or scripts.

11.heading: Productive by design
11.text: Aspire is designed to make distributed systems feel as approachable and productive as a single application.

12.heading: Full lifecycle
12.text: Aspire is a code-first platform that brings the full lifecycle of distributed applications into your workflow.

13.heading: Built in the open
13.text: Aspire is an open-source developer platform for orchestrating complete application stacks together in the open.

14.heading: Cloud-native stack
14.text: Aspire is a cloud-native application stack for building, running, observing, and shipping modern apps today.

15.heading: Application topology
15.text: Aspire is a way to define your entire app topology, from services and databases to every dependency, in code.

16.heading: Unified toolchain
16.text: Aspire is a unified toolchain for running, observing, testing, and deploying multi-service applications.

17.heading: Connected components
17.text: Aspire is an orchestration layer that connects application components and manages how they run together.

18.heading: Production parity
18.text: Aspire is a local-first development model that mirrors production environments without changing your app.

19.heading: Local development
19.text: Aspire is a way to start your entire distributed system locally, with its dependencies, in one command.

20.heading: Less infrastructure
20.text: Aspire is a platform that removes infrastructure friction so you can spend more time building application code.

21.heading: Bring your stack
21.text: Aspire is modular, extensible, and polyglot, so your languages, frameworks, and infrastructure stay yours.

22.heading: Built-in observability
22.text: Aspire is an observability layer with built-in logs, metrics, traces, and health checks ready from the start.

23.heading: Deploy anywhere
23.text: Aspire is environment-agnostic, running locally and deploying to clouds, Kubernetes, or on-premises systems.

24.heading: Topology as code
24.text: Aspire is infrastructure as code for your application topology, expressed in the programming language you use.

25.heading: One workflow
25.text: Aspire is orchestration, observability, and deployment combined in code without stitching together tools.

26.heading: Simpler systems
26.text: Aspire is a developer-focused abstraction that makes distributed systems easier to build, debug, and manage.

27.heading: Architecture in code
27.text: Aspire is your entire app architecture, defined in code and ready to run locally or publish for deployment.

28.heading: Distributed apps
28.text: Aspire is a developer-first platform that gives distributed applications a consistent model and workflow.

29.heading: Developer workflow
29.text: Aspire is orchestration, observability, and deployment built directly into your everyday development workflow.

environments.testText: Spin up and tear down complete staging environments for testing

resources-graph.label: Resource graph
resources-graph.alt: Aspire dashboard resource graph showing the AspireShop services, databases, and caches connected as healthy nodes.

resources-table.label: Resource dashboard
resources-table.alt: Aspire dashboard resources table listing every running project, container, and executable with state, endpoints, and actions.

console.label: Console logs
console.alt: Aspire dashboard console logs streaming interleaved, color-coded output from every resource in real time.

structured-logs.label: Structured logs
structured-logs.alt: Aspire dashboard structured logs view with a filterable, searchable grid of OpenTelemetry log records.

traces.label: Distributed traces
traces.alt: Aspire dashboard traces list showing distributed traces with per-resource span counts and durations.

trace-detail.label: Trace detail
trace-detail.alt: Aspire dashboard trace detail waterfall for an add-to-cart request across the frontend, basket service, and cache, with span attributes.

metrics.label: Metrics
metrics.alt: Aspire dashboard metrics view charting HTTP server request duration percentiles for the frontend with dimension filters.

aspire.verifyInstallation: Verify the installation by checking the Aspire CLI version
aspire.verifyInstallationText: You should see the version number of the Aspire CLI, which indicates that it's installed correctly.
aspire.helpCommand: To see the available commands and options, run the help command:

package.interactiveCli: The Aspire CLI is interactive, be sure to select the appropriate search result when prompted:
package.autoCliSelection: If there's only one result, the CLI selects it automatically. You'll still need to confirm the version.

integrations.noResults: Try searching for things like "SQL", "Cache", or "Redis" to discover integrations!

404.message: — This page doesn't exist—or at least, not anymore. Perhaps the route was deprecated...or maybe it never existed at all?

siteTour.unavailable: Site tour is available on larger screens

tooltips.restart: Start the site tour over from the beginning
tooltips.firstStep: You are on the first step

hint.scrollToTop: Click the highlighted button to return to the top and close the tour.

sidebarToggle.title: Show or hide the sidebar
sidebarToggle.body: Use this control to collapse the sidebar for more reading space, or bring it back when you need the page and section navigation.

topicsList.title: Jump to another doc area
topicsList.body: Switch between top-level doc areas from this list without leaving the docs layout.

search.title: Search the docs
search.body: Search guides, integrations, and reference pages from anywhere.

installCli.title: View CLI install commands
installCli.body: Open this modal to copy install commands for the Aspire CLI without leaving the page.

cookiePreferences.title: Manage cookie consent
cookiePreferences.body: Open this dialog to review or change your cookie consent choices.

tourHelp.title: Reopen the tour
tourHelp.body: Use this help button to restart the tour or open any new tips later.

pageActions.title: Use page actions
pageActions.body: Use these actions to copy this page as Markdown, share it, or send it to an AI tool.

pivotSelector.title: Switch code examples
pivotSelector.body: On pages with multiple code paths, use this selector to switch between the available versions. If it becomes sticky while you scroll, the arrow controls can collapse it or bring it back.

editPage.title: Edit this page on GitHub
editPage.body: Open this page's source on GitHub if you want to suggest a correction or contribute an update.

footerPreferences.title: Change site preferences
footerPreferences.body: Use these footer controls to change the theme, language, or keyboard shortcut conventions.

scrollToTop.title: Back to top
scrollToTop.body: Use this button to return to the top of the page. If the tour is open, clicking it also closes the tour.

appHostBuilder.heading: Build your {{appHost}}
appHostBuilder.description: Toggle different features on/off to see how Aspire defines different parts of your stack.
